public_goals: Reach a practical resolution on the shared debt without requiring forensic accounting.
Confirm that the housing cost arrangement they both remember is what gets formalised.
Move forward quickly enough to avoid carrying joint credit liability longer than necessary.
private_concerns: Believes the informal debt arrangements were clear at the time and worries the other party is now revisiting them strategically.
Is genuinely uncertain whether their recollection of the housing cost split is fully accurate.
Worries that demanding full documentation is a delay tactic rather than a genuine need.
red_lines: Will not accept a process that runs indefinitely because the other party wants perfect documentation.
Will not agree to a split that is materially worse than what they remember the original arrangement to be.
emotional_triggers: documentation requests being treated as evidence of bad faith
their version of the shared arrangement being questioned without basis
feeling treated as if they are trying to cheat the process
disclosure_tendencies: acknowledges uncertainty about exact figures once the process feels non-adversarial
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes more defensive if documentation requests are framed as adversarial gatekeeping
opens up once documentation is framed as a shared clarity tool rather than an accusation
starting_positions: Primary position: the credit-card debt split should follow the mutual understanding they already have.
Secondary position: if documentation is needed, it should be proportional to the amounts at issue.
likely_openings: We already talked about this and had an understanding.
I am open to looking at statements if that helps, but I do not want this to drag on.
